content.subject: Fix 4: apply description guard to update-skill step-06-write
content.description: skf-update-skill/step-06-write.md:193 runs skill-check check --fix without the guard. Add §0 Description Guard Protocol (mirror from create-skill step-06-validate) and wrap the --fix invocation.

content.subject: Fix 3: tighten sanitizer regex against inline generics
content.description: Current regex matches TypeScript generics like Array<T> when not inside backticks. Require the < to be preceded by whitespace or string start (i.e., only match when token is standalone). One-line spec adjustment in step-05 §2a.

content.subject: Fix 6: add description_guard evidence-report slot
content.description: §0 guard protocol logs description_guard_restored but evidence-report template in skill-sections.md has no structured slot. Add ## Description Guard subsection with {restored, tool, original_preserved} fields.

content.subject: Fix 7: extend validate-file-refs.js to check Data: frontmatter keys
content.description: Frontmatter variables like tesslDismissalData, assemblyRulesData, skillSectionsData are unvalidated. Add pattern to STEP_META or new regex in tools/validate-file-refs.js that catches <name>Data: 'assets/...' and resolves relative to workflow root (two dirs up from step file).

content.subject: Review: schema & contract regression
content.description: Reviewer 3 focuses on data-shape contracts: brief scope.amendments schema addition, provenance-map bounded-scan flag, tessl-dismissal-rules file load pattern, description guard protocol. Does any consumer rely on the old shape?

content.subject: Review: cross-workflow regression
content.description: Reviewer 2 finds every dependency on the OLD shape of things that changed (-skill staging dir, step-06 prose behavior, audit-skill scan semantics, brief schema) across workflows I did NOT touch, plus tests, tools, and docs.

content.subject: Fix 5: pre-filter §2 subprocesses against §1b-promoted paths
content.description: §2 Category A launches parallel subprocesses that may double-count §1b-promoted paths. Add explicit pre-filter: build already_added set from §1b promotions, pass as parameter to subprocesses, skip any path in the set.

content.subject: Fix 1: renumber §2c → §2a in step-03-extract.md
content.description: Rename §2c Discovered Authoritative Files → §2a; §2a Resolve Source Access → §2b; §2b Component Library Delegation → §2c. Update internal refs in step-03-extract (line 201, self-refs at 101, 121). Update §2c references in step-03-extract, step-02-detect-changes, skill-brief-schema, step-03d. Compat flag for any external refs.

content.subject: Review: per-commit correctness
content.description: Reviewer 1 traces each of the 7 commits and verifies each edit actually accomplishes its stated goal without logic errors, numbering inconsistencies, or incomplete changes.

content.subject: Fix 2: route promoted authoritative docs to file_entries
content.description: Blocker. Promoted .txt files silently fall through §4 code extraction and never land in provenance map. Fix: §2a (renamed) adds promoted files to authoritative_docs_inventory[], NOT to filtered file list. Step-07 converts inventory to file_entries[] with file_type: "doc". Step-04 merge and audit tracking both pick them up from provenance map naturally.

content.text: Also update step-03 re-extract in update-skill to defensively skip any doc-type entries that might leak through.

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(skf-create-skill): route promoted authoritative docs to file_entries

Review found that promoted authoritative files (llms.txt, AGENTS.md,
etc.) were added to the filtered file list in §2a and would then fall
through §4 AST extraction, which silently produces no exports on non-
code content. The file would be "promoted" but never actually land in
the provenance map — update-skill wouldn't track it, audit-skill's
bounded scan wouldn't see it, and re-running create-skill would
re-trigger the same silent failure. The whole #116 chain was cosmetic
for the exact .txt files it was designed to handle.

Fix by routing docs out of the code-extraction pipeline entirely:

Create-skill changes:

  - step-03 §2a Promote now writes to an in-context promoted_docs[]
    list {path, heuristic, size_bytes, line_count, content_hash}
    instead of the filtered file list. SHA-256 is computed at
    promotion time.
  - §2a "already in scope" case also moves matching heuristic files
    from the filtered list into promoted_docs[], so a manually-
    authored scope.include entry for llms.txt doesn't ghost through
    §4 either.
  - §2a "pre-decided from amendments" case populates promoted_docs[]
    on replay so re-runs deterministically rebuild file_entries
    without re-prompting.
  - step-05 §6 reads promoted_docs[] and emits one file_entries[]
    row per entry with file_type: "doc", extraction_method:
    "promoted-authoritative".
  - step-07 §2 documents that file_type: "doc" entries are NOT
    copied into the skill package (unlike scripts and assets) —
    the source file stays at its original path; only the provenance
    entry is written.
  - step-03d prerequisite message updated to reflect the new routing.

Update-skill changes:

  - step-02 §1b [P] Promote writes to promoted_docs_new[] instead of
    marking the file ADDED in the change manifest. Content hash is
    computed at promotion time.
  - §1b "pre-decided promoted" replay case also populates
    promoted_docs_new[] when a promoted path is missing from the
    provenance map.
  - §1b exclusion set for §2 Category A now sources from
    promoted_docs_new[] (replaces the earlier "already marked ADDED"
    dedup note).
  - step-03 re-extract builds a skip set from promoted_docs_new[] +
    existing file_entries[] with file_type: "doc" and bypasses
    them before AST extraction — defensive guard against any doc
    path leaking into the change manifest.
  - step-04 merge Priority 6 now branches on file_type:
    script/asset behaves as before (file copy + hash update);
    doc updates only the content_hash with no file copy, and
    deletion removes only the provenance entry with no file removal.
  - step-04 merge gains Priority 7 which reads promoted_docs_new[]
    and writes new file_entries[] rows for each entry, no file copy.

Schema changes:

  - assets/skill-sections.md file_entries schema now documents
    the file_type enum (script | asset | doc) with a table covering
    source inventory, whether copied, extraction_method, and purpose.
  - file_name convention for doc type uses a synthetic
    "docs/authoritative/{source_path}" namespace so tools iterating
    file_entries[] by file_name can distinguish docs from scripts
    or assets without inspecting file_type.
  - confidence: "T1-low" for doc type (promotion is source-level
    but content is not AST-parsed).

End-to-end: a promoted llms.txt now lands in provenance-map.json as
a file_entries[] row, drift is detected by the existing Category D
hash comparison, and audit-skill's bounded re-index includes the
path naturally via file_entries[].source_file. Zero changes needed
in audit-skill or the existing Category D code.

Review companion to the 7-commit LTS hardening batch. This is the
most important of the review fixes — without it, the #116 fix was
incomplete for non-code authoritative files.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF

input.old_string:
**Detection:** match any substring of the form `<token>` where `token` contains only letters, digits, hyphens, underscores, or dots (e.g., `<name>`, `<component-id>`, `<file.ts>`). Do NOT match email addresses, URLs with `<` / `>`, or tokens containing spaces.

**Substitution (in priority order):**

1. **Backticked form** — wrap the entire angle-bracket token in backticks: `<name>` → `` `<name>` ``. Preferred for CLI placeholders and example phrasing because it preserves visual intent.
2. **If the token already sits inside a backtick span**, leave it alone — the backticks already protect it from XML-tag parsing. Example: `` `npx foo add <name>` `` is safe as-is.
input.new_string:
**Detection:** match any substring of the form `<token>` where ALL of these conditions hold:

1. `token` contains only letters, digits, hyphens, underscores, or dots (e.g., `<name>`, `<component-id>`, `<file.ts>`).
2. The character immediately before `<` is NOT an ASCII letter or digit. This excludes TypeScript / C++ / Rust generics such as `Array<T>`, `Promise<string>`, `Vec<u8>` where the `<` is attached to an identifier. Inline generics are NOT XML tags — validators do not reject them — and wrapping them in backticks would corrupt the surrounding prose into broken markdown.
3. The token does not contain whitespace (e.g., `<unsigned int>` is a type expression, not a placeholder — skip).
4. The angle-bracket substring is NOT already inside a backtick span (`` `...` ``). Backticks already protect the content from XML-tag parsing. Example: `` `npx foo add <name>` `` is safe as-is.

**Anchor positions where the rule IS intended to match:** start of the description, after a space, after punctuation (`.`, `,`, `;`, `:`, `(`, `[`, `/`, `-`, newline). These are the positions where a standalone placeholder token typically appears.

**Anchor positions where the rule is NOT intended to match:** immediately after a word character (generics position). This is the discriminator between "standalone `<name>` placeholder" and "inline `Array<T>` generic."

**Substitution:**

For each match that passes the detection rules above, wrap the entire angle-bracket token in backticks: `<name>` → `` `<name>` ``. This is the only substitution — no uppercase renaming, no curly-brace conversion. The backtick form preserves visual intent and is invariant under future tessl / skill-check rule changes (backticked content is always literal).

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix(skf-create-skill): tighten sanitizer regex against inline generics

The step-05 §2a sanitizer rule as originally specified matched any
<token> where token was [A-Za-z0-9._-]+. Review caught that this
false-positives on TypeScript, C++, and Rust generics inline in
prose — e.g., a description reading "Returns an Array<T> of results"
would be mangled into "Returns an `<T>` of results", broken markdown
with lost meaning, because the sanitizer cannot tell a standalone
placeholder from a trailing type parameter.

Tighten the detection rules to require all of:

  1. token contains only [A-Za-z0-9._-]+ (unchanged)
  2. character immediately before < is NOT a word character (new —
     this excludes Array<T>, Promise<string>, Vec<u8> because the
     < is attached to an identifier)
  3. token contains no whitespace (excludes type expressions like
     <unsigned int>)
  4. match is not already inside a backtick span (unchanged)

Document anchor positions where the rule IS intended to match
(start of string, after space/punctuation) versus where it is NOT
(after a word character). The discriminator is whether the < is
standalone or attached to an identifier — XML validators only care
about the former; inline generics are not XML tags and do not
trigger validator failures.

Simplify the substitution policy to a single rule: wrap the token
in backticks. No uppercase renaming, no curly-brace conversion.
Backticked content is invariant under future tessl / skill-check
rule changes, making this the LTS-stable choice.

Review companion to the 7-commit LTS hardening batch.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
